# inbound-lead-dedupe-merge — generated workflow

> Generated by the Workflow Press from the frozen contract (version 1).
> Do not edit the generated Go by hand — update the contract and regenerate.

**Goal.** For each new inbound lead, find a matching contact or company; if a match is found, merge idempotently into the existing record; otherwise create a new one.

**Operator.** revops

## States

- `received` (initial) — A new inbound lead has arrived.
- `matched` — Candidate matches have been resolved against existing records.
- `merged` (terminal) — A match was found and the lead was merged idempotently into the existing record.
- `created` (terminal) — No match was found and a new record was created.

## Transitions

- `received` → `matched` on **lead_received** (external)
- `matched` → `merged` on **match_found** (internal) guarded by `has_confident_match`
- `matched` → `created` on **no_match** (internal) guarded by `no_confident_match`

## Actions

- **find_matches** (read) on `lead_received` → crm
- **merge_into_existing** (internal-write) on `match_found` → crm — requires approval — idempotent
- **create_new_record** (internal-write) on `no_match` → crm — requires approval — idempotent

## Exceptions

- **ambiguous_match**: when two or more candidates tie above the match threshold — do not auto-merge; raise a manual-merge decision to the operator with both candidates
- **duplicate_event**: when the same inbound lead event is delivered more than once — absorb the duplicate; the merge/create actions are idempotent so re-running does not double-apply

## SLAs

- **dedupe_latency**: time from lead received to merged/created ≤ 2m

## Verification scenarios

- **matching_lead_merges_idempotently** — fire `lead_received` (expects approval); expects 2 transition(s)
- **new_lead_creates_record** — fire `lead_received` (expects approval); expects 2 transition(s)
- **duplicate_event_does_not_double_apply** — fire `match_found` (expects approval); expects 1 transition(s)

## Execution safety

Actions run through the Workflow Press Executor seam. In this phase the only
backend is the fail-closed host stub: every external write is recorded as **gated**
and is never performed live. The durable adapter (`inngest.go`) is
behavior-equivalent to the local runtime.
